_comment: Golden values from the PAPER'S environment — the yardstick for the tool's
'same engine as the preregistered simulation study' claim.
Measured 2026-07-15 on the machine that produced the confirmatory run, in the
exact pinned environment recorded in repro/versions.tsv:
Why fixtures and not a live native run: the local R has since moved to 4.6.1,
and current-native DIVERGES from the paper's environment at the sparse corner
(conv_rate 0.560 vs 0.570) even at the same lme4 version — the numerical
environment, not the package version, moves the marginal fits. So a live
native run is not a stable reference. These recorded numbers are.
Regenerate ONLY from a restored R 4.3.1 + repro/versions.tsv environment
(or the Dockerfile, which pins exactly that). Do not regenerate from whatever
R happens to be installed — that would silently redefine the claim.

cells.label: sparse  N=20 D=7  rate=1
cells.note: the sparse corner: convergence is marginal here, which makes it the sensitive case for H4 and the one that catches numerical drift

cells.label: typical N=60 D=14 rate=2
cells.note: a comfortably-converging design; matches across every environment tested so far
